title: NEE vs. AEP: Which Power Utility Offers Stronger Investment Potential?
summary: This article compares NextEra Energy (NEE) and American Electric Power (AEP), two leading U.S. electric utilities, to determine which offers stronger investment potential. It highlights their financials, investment in clean energy, dividend yields, debt-to-capital ratios, and capital expenditure plans. Ultimately, the article concludes that NextEra Energy has an edge due to stronger earnings estimate revisions, a higher return on equity, and broader capital expenditure plans.

title: AEP, SoftBank Ink Deal with US DOE for 10 GW Data Center Power
summary: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) has partnered with American Electric Power Co (AEP) and a consortium led by Japan's Softbank Group Corp to build 10 GW of power generation, including 9.2 GW from natural gas, on DOE land. This power will supply a 10 GW data center in Pike County, Ohio, requiring AEP to construct a 765-kilovolt transmission infrastructure with Softbank's SB Energy committing $4.2 billion. The initiative is part of the U.S.-Japan Strategic Trade and Investment Agreement, underscoring significant investment in energy infrastructure and technological development in the U.S.

title: Cyndeo Wealth Partners LLC Has $7.37 Million Stock Holdings in American Electric Power Company, Inc. $AEP
summary: Cyndeo Wealth Partners LLC significantly increased its stake in American Electric Power (AEP) by 983% in Q4, acquiring an additional 57,977 shares to reach a total holding of 63,875 shares valued at $7.37 million. American Electric Power reported strong Q4 results, surpassing analyst expectations with $1.19 EPS and $5.32 billion in revenue, and provided an optimistic FY2026 EPS guidance of $6.15-$6.45. The company maintains a quarterly dividend of $0.95, reflecting a 3.0% yield, and benefits from a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $136.10.

title: Why Vistra Stock Price Fell 12.6% as AI-Power Shares Tumbled
summary: Vistra Corp (NYSE: VST) stock price plummeted 12.6% on Friday, closing at $146.02, as AI-related power stocks broadly declined. This widespread dip, affecting companies like Constellation Energy, Talen Energy, and NRG Energy, was influenced by an overall tough day on Wall Street, with the S&P 500 experiencing its weakest finish in six months, and utilities taking a significant hit. Despite Vistra's recent rating upgrade to investment grade and its strong position in the AI-fueled data center demand, broader market anxieties over inflation, interest rate hikes, and rising power-purchase agreement costs contributed to the sell-off.

title: TruWealth Advisors Purchases $8.3M Stake in American Electric Power
summary: TruWealth Advisors LLC has acquired a new stake of 72,511 shares in American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) during the fourth quarter of 2025, valued at approximately $8.36 million. This investment signals continued institutional confidence in American Electric Power's long-term growth potential as a major utility company. The purchase highlights the utility sector's attractiveness to institutional investors like TruWealth Advisors.

title: AEP Taps Piketon Transmission Build To Power Massive Data Center Growth
summary: American Electric Power Company (AEP) is involved in a multi-billion dollar investment to build advanced transmission infrastructure at the former Piketon gaseous diffusion plant in Appalachian Ohio. This project aims to power a planned 10-gigawatt data center campus and support economic development without increasing rates for Ohio residents. The initiative highlights AEP's role in addressing the growing power needs of data centers and the potential for regulated utilities to collaborate with public agencies.

title: Union Bancaire Privee UBP SA Raises Stake in American Electric Power Company, Inc. $AEP
summary: Union Bancaire Privee UBP SA significantly increased its stake in American Electric Power (NASDAQ:AEP) by 267.0% in Q4, acquiring an additional 16,205 shares, bringing its total holdings to 22,275 shares valued at approximately $2.57 million. American Electric Power reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations with an EPS of $1.19 and revenue of $5.32 billion, and also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.95 per share. Analysts generally have a "Moderate Buy" rating for AEP, with a consensus price target of $135.90, even as some insider transactions involved the sale of shares by company EVPs.

title: 72,511 Shares in American Electric Power Company, Inc. $AEP Purchased by TruWealth Advisors LLC
summary: TruWealth Advisors LLC recently acquired 72,511 shares of American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP) in Q4, valued at approximately $8.36 million. This purchase is part of a broader trend where institutional investors, including Victory Capital Management and Soroban Capital Partners, have significantly increased their stakes, bringing total institutional ownership to 75.24%. American Electric Power has reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates, and announced a $3.80 annual dividend plan.

title: Union Bancaire Privee UBP SA Increases Stake in American Electric Power
summary: Union Bancaire Privee UBP SA significantly increased its stake in American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP) by 267% in Q4 2025, now holding 22,275 shares valued at $2.57 million. This move indicates the Swiss private banking group's confidence in AEP's long-term value, growth prospects, and ability to provide stable returns as a major regulated utility in the United States. The investment highlights the continued appeal of utilities as income-generating assets for institutional investors.

title: How Investors May Respond To NextEra Energy (NEE) Massive US Japan Gas Build Out Approval
summary: NextEra Energy (NEE) has received approval for a significant 10-gigawatt natural gas-powered generation build-out in Texas and Pennsylvania, backed by a US-Japan trade deal and US$550 billion investment from Japan. This project, while reinforcing NextEra's growth story, introduces execution, permitting, and financing risks, especially given existing capital plans and rising interest costs. Investors should weigh the potential for growth against increased leverage and project-related uncertainties.

title: CIBC Bancorp USA Inc. Buys New Position in American Electric Power Company, Inc. $AEP
summary: CIBC Bancorp USA Inc. has acquired a new position of 93,343 shares in American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP), valued at approximately $10.5 million during the third quarter. Other institutional investors like Victory Capital Management, Soroban Capital Partners, AQR Capital Management, Ameriprise Financial, and ANTIPODES PARTNERS also significantly increased their holdings in AEP. The article also notes recent insider stock sales, several analyst rating changes, and details AEP's latest financial performance and dividend announcement.

title: American Electric Power stock faces pressure amid utility sector volatility and rate concerns
summary: American Electric Power (AEP) stock experienced a sharp decline on NASDAQ due to broader utility sector weakness and concerns over interest rates and regulatory hurdles. Despite offering reliable dividends and a strategic focus on clean energy, the stock's volatility highlights risks associated with interest rates and regulatory approvals. DACH investors are advised to monitor these factors, with AEP strategically positioned to benefit from energy transition tailwinds and data center demand, making it a defensive play alongside these risks.

title: American Electric Power Co. Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: American Electric Power Co. Inc. (AEP) stock fell by 2.38% to $125.66 on Friday, marking its fourth consecutive day of losses. This dip occurred during a broad market downturn, with both the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also recording losses. Despite the daily decline, the article's title suggests AEP still outperformed competitors.

title: AEP Ohio Partners to Bring $4.2B in New Electric Infrastructure in Appalachian Ohio Without Raising Customer Rates
summary: AEP Ohio, the U.S. Department of Energy, and SB Energy announced a $4.2 billion investment in new electric infrastructure, including 765-kilovolt transmission lines, to support data center growth in Appalachian Ohio. This project, which includes a 10-gigawatt data center campus in Piketon, Ohio, will be funded by SB Energy, ensuring that AEP Ohio customers will not experience increased transmission rates. The new infrastructure is critical for economic development and is expected to begin powering the site by 2029.

title: A $4.2B Ohio data center grid plan aims to avoid rate hikes
summary: AEP Ohio, in partnership with the U.S. Department of Energy and SB Energy, announced a $4.2 billion investment to build new 765-kV transmission infrastructure in Appalachian Ohio. This infrastructure will support a planned 10-gigawatt data center campus at the former Piketon gaseous diffusion plant. SB Energy committed to fund the $4.2 billion to prevent residential rate hikes, with power expected to flow by 2029, pending regulatory approvals.

title: Florida power customers can get mid-cycle bill estimates before heat rises
summary: Duke Energy Florida is promoting its Usage Alerts program to help customers monitor electricity use and anticipate monthly bills, especially as summer heat increases energy consumption. Customers with smart meters and an email address are automatically enrolled, receiving mid-cycle estimates to allow adjustments and avoid bill surprises. This initiative aims to provide billing visibility and support customers in managing their energy costs.

title: FirstEnergy Corp stock rises on strong 2025 earnings and upbeat 2026 guidance amid utility sector momentum
summary: FirstEnergy Corp (ISIN: US31428X1063) shares have risen 3.5% following its latest earnings report, driven by stronger-than-expected Q4 2025 results and a robust 2026 outlook. The utility surpassed Q4 operating earnings estimates and reported a 7.6% increase in full-year adjusted EPS for 2025. With promising 2026 core EPS guidance, increased capital spending for grid modernization, and an attractive dividend yield, FirstEnergy is drawing investor interest, particularly from DACH investors seeking stable US energy yields.

title: NextEra Energy receives approval from President Donald J. Trump to develop up to 10 GW of natural gas‑powered generation to meet nation's historic power demand
summary: NextEra Energy has received approval from President Donald J. Trump to develop up to 10 gigawatts of natural gas-powered generation in Texas and Pennsylvania. This development is part of Japan's $550 billion investment commitment to the United States and aims to meet growing electricity demand, strengthen the U.S. industrial base, and support large-scale users like data centers. NextEra Energy's CEO, John Ketchum, stated that the projects are designed to scale quickly and enhance America's energy security without increasing electricity costs for households.

title: FirstEnergy Corp stock rises on strong 2025 earnings and upbeat 2026 guidance amid utility sector mo
summary: FirstEnergy Corp (ISIN: US31428X1063) shares have risen by 3.5% following strong Q4 2025 earnings and optimistic 2026 guidance, surpassing analyst expectations. The utility reported operating earnings of 53 cents per share for Q4 2025 and a full-year adjusted EPS of $2.55, with 2026 core EPS guidance set between $2.62-$2.82. The company plans significant capital investments of $36 billion from 2026-2030, aimed at grid modernization and transmission upgrades, appealing to investors seeking stable returns and dividend yields.

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Utilities Companies: DTE Energy (DTE) and Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG)
summary: Two analysts have issued bullish sentiments for Utilities sector companies DTE Energy (DTE) and Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG). UBS analyst William Appicelli maintained a Buy rating on DTE Energy with a $167.00 price target, while Bank of America Securities analyst Ross Fowler maintained a Buy rating on Constellation Energy Corporation. Both companies show a consensus of Buy ratings from analysts, with predicted upsides from current levels.

title: Dorian LPG takes delivery of dual-fuel LPG and ammonia carrier
summary: Dorian LPG has taken delivery of the "Areion," a new 93,000 cbm dual-fuel LPG and ammonia gas carrier from Hanwha Ocean. This vessel, which can run on LPG or fuel oil, significantly reduces CO2 emissions and is equipped with a hybrid scrubber, Alternative Marine Power, and is Battery Energy Storage System-ready. The delivery expands Dorian LPG's low-emissions fleet and was financed through a $62.9 million loan from Citibank and Nordea.

title: AEP Ohio Partners to Bring $4.2B in New Electric Infrastructure in Appalachian Ohio Without Raising Customer Rates
summary: AEP Ohio, in partnership with the U.S. Department of Energy and SB Energy, announced plans for a $4.2 billion investment in new electric transmission infrastructure in Appalachian Ohio. This investment will support a 10-gigawatt data center campus in Piketon and is designed to boost economic development without increasing transmission rates for Ohio residents, as SB Energy will cover the costs. The project involves new 765-kilovolt (kV) transmission lines, with power expected to flow by 2029, and AEP Ohio will engage with the community for route planning and regulatory approvals.

title: Hudson Bay Capital Management LP Trims Stock Position in American Electric Power Company, Inc. $AEP
summary: Hudson Bay Capital Management LP significantly reduced its stake in American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP) by 52.2% in the third quarter of the previous year. Despite this, other institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc., State Street Corp, and Invesco Ltd. increased their positions in AEP during the same period. The company also announced a quarterly dividend and received various analyst ratings, with a consensus of "Moderate Buy" and a target price of $135.90.

title: Steadtrust LLC Purchases New Holdings in American Electric Power Company, Inc. $AEP
summary: Steadtrust LLC has acquired a new stake in American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP), purchasing 4,650 shares valued at approximately $523,000. This investment reflects Steadtrust's confidence in AEP's long-term growth potential and its position in the evolving energy landscape, as AEP continues to diversify its asset portfolio with renewable energy.

title: Constellation Energy (NASDAQ: CEG) 2026 proxy outlines Calpine deal, TSR and pay
summary: Constellation Energy's 2026 proxy statement details upcoming shareholder votes, including the election of directors and advisory approval of executive compensation. The company highlights its strong performance since separating from Exelon in 2022, boasting a 634% total shareholder return through 2025, and details the strategic acquisition of Calpine Corporation, which closed on January 7, 2026, expanding its clean energy fleet. The proxy also outlines Constellation's capital allocation plan, commitment to annual dividend growth, share repurchase program, and a performance-based executive compensation structure heavily tied to financial and operational goals.

title: Edison International Opens Applications for Lineworker Scholarship
summary: Edison International has launched its 2026 Lineworker Scholarship program, offering up to $25,000 to 12 recipients. The scholarship, developed with IBEW Local 47, provides training, tuition, and support services for a career in the energy and utility industry, with a focus on roles at Southern California Edison. The program aims to address the growing need for skilled lineworkers, with applications open until May 8.

title: American Electric Power Co. Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors
summary: American Electric Power Co. Inc. (AEP) stock fell 1.72% on Thursday, closing at $128.72. This underperformance occurred during a generally negative trading session, with the S&P 500 Index dropping 0.27% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average falling 0.44%. This marks the third consecutive day of losses for AEP's stock.

title: York Water’s March Price Increase May Spark Volatility and Create Compounding Opportunities for Small-Cap Utilities
summary: York Water, a small-cap utility with a long history of dividends, recently secured a 14% rate increase, projected to generate an additional $18.9 million in annual revenue. While this boosts cash flow, the company's significant capital reinvestments ($48 million annually) and its small market capitalization at $455 million make it susceptible to volatility. Investors need to weigh the reliability of its regulated cash flows against the risks of its size and the need for future regulatory approvals to justify its current premium valuation.

title: Wall Street Analysts Are Bullish on Top Utilities Picks
summary: Wall Street analysts are showing bullish sentiments towards the Utilities sector, specifically highlighting Oklo Inc (OKLO) and DTE Energy (DTE). H.C. Wainwright maintained a Buy rating on Oklo Inc with a $90.00 price target, while Wells Fargo maintained a Buy rating on DTE Energy with a $152.00 price target. Both companies received Moderate Buy consensus ratings from analysts.

title: Mainstay Capital Management LLC ADV Sells 15,866 Shares of American Electric Power Company, Inc. $AEP
summary: Mainstay Capital Management LLC ADV significantly reduced its stake in American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) by selling 15,866 shares, decreasing its holdings by 50.6% during the third quarter. Despite this, other institutional investors have either acquired new positions or increased their stakes in AEP. The article also details recent insider stock sales, AEP's stock performance, dividend announcement, and recent analyst ratings, which generally indicate a "Moderate Buy" consensus.

title: Fulcrum Asset Management LLP Purchases 8,127 Shares of American Electric Power Company, Inc. $AEP
summary: Fulcrum Asset Management LLP significantly increased its stake in American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP) during the third quarter, purchasing 8,127 shares to bring its total holdings to 15,927 shares valued at $1.79 million. This increase reflects growing institutional interest, as other hedge funds also adjusted their positions in AEP. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations, and announced a quarterly dividend, while several analysts have issued positive ratings and price targets.

title: NextEra Energy, Inc. Experiences Revision in Its Stock Evaluation Amidst Market Dynamics
summary: NextEra Energy, Inc. has undergone a valuation adjustment, showing a P/E ratio of 42 and a price-to-book value of 3.44. The company reports a high dividend yield of 256.72%, with a ROCE of 4.83% and an ROE of 8.21%, indicating strong performance. These metrics position NextEra with notably higher valuations compared to many industry peers in the power sector.

title: Analysts’ Top Energy Picks: Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI), Schlumberger (SLB)
summary: Two energy sector companies, Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) and Schlumberger (SLB), recently received bullish ratings from analysts. Morgan Stanley maintained a Buy rating on Solaris with a $72.00 target, while Barclays maintained a Buy rating on Schlumberger with a $49.00 target. Both companies have an analyst consensus of "Strong Buy" with significant upside potential from current levels.

title: Niskanen Center examines American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP)’s place in the US transmission grid
summary: The Niskanen Center has published a report analyzing American Electric Power Company, Inc.'s (AEP) role and importance within the U.S. electricity transmission grid. The report likely delves into AEP's infrastructure, operational impact, and its significance for the nation's energy future and grid reliability.

title: Is Duke Energy (DUK) Priced Reasonably After Recent Share Gains And Mixed Valuation Signals
summary: This article analyzes whether Duke Energy (DUK) is reasonably priced after recent share gains using two valuation methods. The Dividend Discount Model suggests the stock is significantly overvalued by 101.4%, while the Price/Earnings ratio indicates it might be undervalued when compared to a proprietary "Fair Ratio." The company's valuation score is 3/6, reflecting mixed signals for investors to consider.

title: Wolfe Research upgrades American Electric Power (AEP)
summary: Wolfe Research has upgraded American Electric Power (AEP) from Peer Perform to Outperform. The firm also set a price target of $92.00 for AEP. This upgrade indicates a more positive outlook for the company's stock from Wolfe Research.

title: Caerus Investment Advisors LLC Invests $882,000 in American Electric Power Company, Inc. $AEP
summary: Caerus Investment Advisors LLC recently acquired a new stake of 7,837 shares, valued at $882,000, in American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP) during the third quarter. AEP reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst estimates with $1.19 EPS and revenue up 13.2% year-over-year, and issued optimistic FY2026 guidance. The company also announced a quarterly dividend, yielding approximately 2.8%, and holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts.

title: Here’s how to report and check on power outages
summary: This article provides essential information for Virginia residents on how to report and check power outages, especially in anticipation of severe storms. It lists specific contact methods and outage maps for major utility providers like Dominion Energy, Rappahannock Electric Co-op, Northern Neck Electric Co-op, Southside Electric Co-op, and Central Virginia Electric Cooperative. The article also directs users to a general outage map for wider coverage.

title: AEP names new president of Appalachian Power
summary: American Electric Power (AEP) has appointed Brian Abraham as the new president and chief operating officer of Appalachian Power. Abraham, formerly chief of staff to West Virginia Senator Jim Justice, will focus on strengthening stakeholder relationships and developing state-focused energy solutions. He succeeds Aaron Walker, who transitions to vice president, Engineering and Quality, Nuclear Development.

title: Ex-senator’s chief of staff tapped to run Appalachian Power for AEP
summary: American Electric Power (AEP) has appointed Brian Abraham as president and chief operating officer of Appalachian Power (APCo), effective April 13, 2026. Abraham, formerly chief of staff to West Virginia Senator Jim Justice, will focus on strengthening stakeholder relationships and developing state-focused energy solutions in West Virginia, Virginia, and Tennessee. Aaron Walker, whom Abraham is succeeding, will transition to serve as vice president, Engineering and Quality, Nuclear Development.

title: Cisco and Daimler join Duke Energy program to source NC renewable power
summary: Duke Energy's GSA Express clean energy program in North Carolina has exceeded initial enrollment targets, subscribing 177.3 MW of renewable capacity. Major participants like Cisco, Daimler Truck North America, and United States Cold Storage Inc. are leveraging the program to meet their sustainability goals. The voluntary initiative provides a streamlined way for large business customers to source up to 100% of their electricity from new renewable facilities on Duke Energy's grid.

title: AEP names Brian Abraham, Sen. Justice's chief of staff, head of Appalachian Power
summary: Brian Abraham, chief of staff for U.S. Sen. Jim Justice, has been appointed president and chief operating officer of Appalachian Power, effective April 13. Abraham previously served as Justice's chief of staff during his second term as West Virginia's governor and followed him to Washington. He replaces Aaron Walker, who moved to a new role within AEP.

title: AEP Names Brian Abraham to Lead Appalachian Power, Aaron Walker Appointed to Nuclear Development Organization Role
summary: American Electric Power (AEP) has appointed Brian Abraham as president and chief operating officer of Appalachian Power (APCo) to enhance stakeholder relationships in its service territory. Abraham, former chief of staff to Senator Jim Justice, will focus on balanced energy solutions and affordability. Aaron Walker, whom Abraham replaces, will transition to vice president, Engineering and Quality, Nuclear Development, contributing his operational expertise to AEP's advanced nuclear solutions.

title: Alabama county extends first responder radio coverage by thousands of miles
summary: Southern Linc, a subsidiary of Southern Company (NYSE: SO), partnered with the Blount County, Alabama Communications District (BCCD) to integrate existing radio networks with its CriticalLinc™ LTE network. This collaboration aims to provide a more reliable and interoperable communication system for public safety employees, expanding radio coverage by thousands of miles. The solution uses a Catalyst gateway to create a unified LMR/LTE network, ensuring enhanced reliability, built-in redundancy, and end-to-end encryption for 32 local agencies without the need for a costly new standalone system.

title: American Electric Power stock hits all-time high at $134.82
summary: American Electric Power (AEP) stock recently reached an all-time high of $134.82, reflecting strong investor confidence and robust performance including a 30.8% return over the past year and 16 consecutive years of dividend increases. Despite being considered overvalued by InvestingPro, the company has seen increased analyst ratings and commitments for large load demand, especially crucial with the rising AI infrastructure growth. AEP also announced a $750 million senior notes offering and appointed a new president and COO for AEP Texas.

title: Bank of Nova Scotia Acquires 141,418 Shares of American Electric Power Company, Inc. $AEP
summary: Bank of Nova Scotia increased its stake in American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) by 129.2% in the third quarter, purchasing an additional 141,418 shares, bringing its total to 250,889 shares worth $28.225 million. The article also details other institutional investors' activities, AEP's recent financial performance including its dividend announcement and Q4 earnings exceeding estimates, and recent insider stock sales by the company's EVPs. Analyst ratings for AEP are predominantly "Buy" or "Hold," with an average price target of $135.90.

title: JPMorgan Raises Price Target on American Electric (AEP)
summary: J.P. Morgan has increased its price target for American Electric Power Company Inc. (AEP) to $139 from $125, while maintaining a Neutral rating, following a review of the North American utilities sector. This action comes after UBS also upgraded AEP from Sell to Neutral, raising its price target to $132 due to expectations of stronger earnings growth and the company's leading position in transmission networks and electricity load contracts. Both firms highlight AEP's solid foundation and potential for long-term growth, although the article also notes a belief that some AI stocks may offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

title: NiSource Q2 Earnings & Revenues Beat Estimates, Rise Y/Y
summary: NiSource (NI) reported strong second-quarter 2024 results, surpassing both earnings and revenue estimates and showing year-over-year growth. The company achieved an operating earnings per share of $0.18, exceeding the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.09, and revenues of $1,059 million, which also beat expectations and rose from the prior year. NiSource's consistent capital investments in infrastructure modernization and safety are contributing to its improved performance and customer service.

title: FirstEnergy Crews Ready for More Severe Weather as Restoration from Windstorm Continues
summary: FirstEnergy Corp. is preparing for another round of severe weather, including strong winds, rain, and potential tornadoes, as restoration efforts continue for over 800,000 customers affected by a previous windstorm. The company has mobilized over 10,000 workers to address outages and urges customers to prioritize safety and report any new power losses. Extensive repairs have been made, and the focus remains on quickly and safely restoring power, starting with critical services and areas affecting the most customers.

title: 5 Best Regulated Electric Stocks to Buy According to Hedge Funds
summary: American Electric Power Company Inc. (NASDAQ: AEP) is highlighted as one of the top regulated electric stocks favored by hedge funds. J.P. Morgan recently increased its price target for AEP to $139, maintaining a Neutral rating, while UBS upgraded the stock from Sell to Neutral, raising its price target to $132. These revisions reflect AEP's strong earnings expansion potential, its significant backlog of electricity load contracts, and its extensive transmission network.
